mode_defaults.h(3) Library Functions Manual mode_defaults.h(3)

NAME

mode_defaults.h - Mode-aware default value getters and mode metadata.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Functions


const void * get_default_log_file (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --log-file. const void * get_default_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --port. const void * get_default_websocket_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --websocket-port. void apply_mode_specific_defaults (options_t *opts)
Apply mode-specific defaults to an options struct after mode detection. const mode_descriptor_t * get_mode_descriptor (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode descriptor by mode. const mode_descriptor_t * get_modes_by_group (const char *group, size_t *out_count)
Get all mode descriptors for a specific group.

Detailed Description

Mode-aware default value getters and mode metadata.

Provides mode-specific default values and metadata (names, descriptions, groups) for all application modes. Used by option parsing and completion generation.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file mode_defaults.h.

Function Documentation

void apply_mode_specific_defaults (options_t * opts)

Apply mode-specific defaults to an options struct after mode detection. Updates options that have mode-dependent defaults (like log_file, port, and websocket_port) based on the detected mode. Should be called after mode detection but before option parsing.

Parameters

opts Options struct to update (must have detected_mode already set)

This function updates options that have mode-dependent defaults (port and websocket_port) based on the detected mode. Log file paths are handled separately by options_get_log_filepath().

Parameters

opts Options struct to update (must have detected_mode already set)

Definition at line 184 of file options/registry/mode_defaults.c.

184                                                    {
185   if (!opts) {
186     return;
187   }
188 
189   // Apply port default based on mode
190   const int *mode_port = (const int *)get_default_port(opts->detected_mode);
191   if (mode_port) {
192     // Only update if still at a generic default (27224 or 27225)
193     if (opts->port == OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT || opts->port == OPT_ACDS_PORT_INT_DEFAULT) {
194       opts->port = *mode_port;
195     }
196   }
197 
198   // Apply websocket-port default based on mode
199   const int *mode_websocket_port = (const int *)get_default_websocket_port(opts->detected_mode);
200   if (mode_websocket_port) {
201     // Only update if still at a generic default (27226 or 27227)
202     if (opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_SERVER_DEFAULT ||
203         opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_ACDS_DEFAULT) {
204       opts->websocket_port = *mode_websocket_port;
205     }
206   }
207 
208   // If TLS cert+key are provided and websocket-port was not explicitly set,
209   // default to 443 (standard HTTPS/WSS port) instead of the mode default.
210   if (opts->websocket_tls_cert[0] != '\0' && opts->websocket_tls_key[0] != '\0') {
211     if (opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_SERVER_DEFAULT ||
212         opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_ACDS_DEFAULT) {
213       opts->websocket_port = 443;
214     }
215   }
216 }




References options_state::detected_mode,
    get_default_port(), get_default_websocket_port(),
    OPT_ACDS_PORT_INT_DEFAULT, OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT,
    OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_ACDS_DEFAULT,
    OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_SERVER_DEFAULT, options_state::port,
    options_state::websocket_port,
    options_state::websocket_tls_cert, and
    options_state::websocket_tls_key.


Referenced by options_init().






const
  void * get_default_log_file (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --log-file.


Parameters


mode The mode to get the default for


Returns


Pointer to the default log file path for this mode


Returns empty string - the actual log file path is determined by
    options_get_log_filepath() which properly handles debug vs release builds
    and platform-specific temp directories.




Debug builds: Uses current working directory (server.log,
    client.log, etc.) Release builds: Uses $TMPDIR/ascii-chat/server.log,
  etc.


Definition at line 109 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


109                                                         {
110   (void)mode; // Unused
111   // Return empty string - let options_get_log_filepath() handle the defaults
112   return "";
113 }










const
  void * get_default_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --port.


Parameters


mode The mode to get the default for


Returns


Pointer to the default port number for this mode


Definition at line 127 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


127                                                     {
128   switch (mode) {
129   case MODE_SERVER:
130     return &g_port_server;
131   case MODE_CLIENT:
132     return &g_port_client;
133   case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
134     return &g_port_discovery_service;
135   case MODE_DISCOVERY:
136     return &g_port_discovery;
137   case MODE_MIRROR:
138   case MODE_INVALID:
139   default:
140     // Fallback
141     return &g_port_server;
142   }
143 }






References MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR, and
    MODE_SERVER.


Referenced by apply_mode_specific_defaults().






const
  void * get_default_websocket_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --websocket-port.


Parameters


mode The mode to get the default for


Returns


Pointer to the default WebSocket port number for this
  mode


Definition at line 155 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


155                                                               {
156   switch (mode) {
157   case MODE_SERVER:
158     return &g_websocket_port_server;
159   case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
160     return &g_websocket_port_discovery_service;
161   case MODE_CLIENT:
162   case MODE_MIRROR:
163   case MODE_DISCOVERY:
164   case MODE_INVALID:
165   default:
166     // Fallback (should not be used for client/mirror/discovery)
167     return &g_websocket_port_server;
168   }
169 }






References MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR, and
    MODE_SERVER.


Referenced by apply_mode_specific_defaults().






const mode_descriptor_t * get_mode_descriptor
  (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode descriptor by mode. Returns metadata (name, description,
    group) for the given mode. Useful for UI generation and help text.




Parameters


mode Mode to look up


Returns


Pointer to mode descriptor, or NULL if mode not
  found


Example








const mode_descriptor_t *desc = get_mode_descriptor(MODE_SERVER);
if (desc) {


    printf("Mode: %s\nDescription: %s\nGroup: %s\n",


           desc->name, desc->description, desc->group);
}





Parameters


mode Mode to look up


Returns


Pointer to mode descriptor, or NULL if not found


Definition at line 41 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


41                                                                     {
42   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name; i++) {
43     if (g_mode_descriptors[i].mode == mode) {
44       return &g_mode_descriptors[i];
45     }
46   }
47   return NULL;
48 }










const mode_descriptor_t * get_modes_by_group (const char *
  group, size_t * out_count)


Get all mode descriptors for a specific group. Returns all modes
    in a given category (e.g., "server-like" or
    "client-like"). The returned array is allocated with SAFE_MALLOC
    and must be freed by caller.




Parameters


group Group name ("server-like" or
  "client-like")


out_count OUTPUT: Number of modes in this group


Returns


Array of mode descriptors (caller must free with
  SAFE_FREE), or NULL if group not found


Example








size_t count;
const mode_descriptor_t *server_modes = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &count);
if (server_modes) {


    for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {


        printf("%s: %s\n", server_modes[i].name, server_modes[i].description);


    }


    SAFE_FREE(server_modes);
}





Get all mode descriptors for a specific group.




Parameters


group Group name ("server-like" or
  "client-like")


out_count OUTPUT: Number of modes in this group


Returns


Array of mode descriptors, or NULL if group not
  found


Definition at line 56 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


56                                                                                   {
57   if (!group || !out_count) {
58     return NULL;
59   }
60 
61   // Count matching modes
62   size_t count = 0;
63   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name; i++) {
64     if (strcmp(g_mode_descriptors[i].group, group) == 0) {
65       count++;
66     }
67   }
68 
69   if (count == 0) {
70     *out_count = 0;
71     return NULL;
72   }
73 
74   // Allocate result array
75   mode_descriptor_t *result = SAFE_MALLOC((count + 1) * sizeof(mode_descriptor_t), mode_descriptor_t *);
76   size_t idx = 0;
77 
78   // Fill result array
79   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name && idx < count; i++) {
80     if (strcmp(g_mode_descriptors[i].group, group) == 0) {
81       result[idx++] = g_mode_descriptors[i];
82     }
83   }
84 
85   // Add terminator
86   result[idx].mode = MODE_INVALID;
87   result[idx].name = NULL;
88   result[idx].description = NULL;
89   result[idx].group = NULL;
90 
91   *out_count = count;
92   return result;
93 }






References mode_descriptor_t::description,
    mode_descriptor_t::group, mode_descriptor_t::mode,
    MODE_INVALID, mode_descriptor_t::name, and
  SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by completions_generate_zsh().
